---
title: "EDA practice lab"
format: html
---

# Load some libraries

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

```

# Load data

```{python}
budget = pd.read_csv("Maryland_Operating_Budget__FY2024.csv")
budget.head()
budget.columns
```

- narrow down columns

```{python}
columns_to_keep = ["Fiscal.Year", "Agency.Name", "Program.Name", "Fund.Type.Name", "Budget", "Type", "Category.Title"]
budget = budget[columns_to_keep]
budget.columns
budget.head()
budget.dtypes
budget.describe()
len(budget)
```


# plot some histograms

```{python}
sns.histplot(budget, x = "Budget")
plt.xlim(-3.0e+8, 3.8e+09)

```


```{python}
budget["log_dollars"] = np.log10(budget["Budget"])

budget["log_dollars"].describe()
budget["Budget"].isna().sum()

budget_zeros = budget[budget["Budget"] == 0]
len(budget_zeros)

budget_neg = budget[budget["Budget"] < 0]
len(budget_neg)

budget_pos = budget[budget["Budget"] > 0]
len(budget_pos)
```

# figure out negative dollar values

- TBD

# Look at positive values

```{python}
sns.histplot(budget_pos, x = "log_dollars")
plt.clf()
```


# Look at some catagory counts


```{python}
budget_pos["Agency.Name"].unique()

budget_pos["Agency.Name"].value_counts()

budget_pos[["Agency.Name", "Budget"]].groupby("Agency.Name").sum().sort_values(by = "Budget", ascending=False)

```
